Who we are

FRIENDS WITH A PRESSURE WASHER HELPING THE NEIGHBORHOOD

ARG is a local group of friends who are seeking to help our neighbors to brighten up their homes. We started this because our own neighborhoods could use more cleaning to help the environment — green walkways, streaked driveways, decks and patios nobody used — and our goal is to ensure homeowners actually feel at home.

We researched the market average in the local area. We can guarantee we are the cheaper option with the best outcome. Move the sliders, see the number, pick a day. When we show up, it's us, the same people every time.

What we clean →

Surfaces we handle

What you're actually calling about

Problems, not pressure washing.

Nobody wants a pressure washer. They want the black stripe gone, the green off the walkway before somebody slips, and the deck usable by your next cookout. That's what we sell — the machine is just how we get there. Almost every job in the 804 comes down to one of these four.

The driveway makes the house look old

Tire marks, oil shadows, and that dark strip down the middle. The house is fine — the concrete in front of it is doing all the aging.

The fix: surface-cleaner pass, then a rinse that pulls the stain out instead of striping it.

Somebody's going to slip

Green film on the walkway or the back steps after a wet Virginia spring. It's algae, it's slick, and it's the reason a lot of folks finally call.

The fix: a treatment that kills the growth at the root, so it isn't back in six weeks.

The deck and patio are dirty and nobody uses them

Wood and composite don't need pressure — they need the right chemistry at low pressure. Blast a deck and you furr the grain permanently.

The fix: a gentle soft wash that brings your boards back to life — no damage, no etching, just a clean surface ready for your next get-together.

It's going on the market

Agent said clean it up, showing is in ten days, and you don't have time to chase three companies for callbacks that never come.

The fix: whole-property bundle, booked online, done in one visit.

Before you book

Some questions to think about

A few honest questions about your home's exterior. No right answers — they just help you figure out whether a cleaning is worth it.

  1. Have you noticed green, black, or gray stains creeping across your driveway, sidewalks, deck, porch or patio?
  2. Would you say your home is the cleanest-looking one on your street?
  3. When was the last time you pressure washed your driveway, sidewalks, deck, porch or patio?
  4. Do you ever feel like your home's exterior doesn't quite match how well you keep the inside?
  5. Did you know it's recommended to pressure wash your surfaces annually to prevent permanent staining and decay?
  6. Would you agree a clean exterior makes a strong first impression on guests or buyers?
  7. What would you expect to pay for an exterior surface cleaning?
  8. Would it bother you to replace a driveway, sidewalk, deck, porch or patio that just needed a good cleaning?
  9. If it were affordable, would you want your home looking brand-new again?
  10. Would you be interested in seeing what your home could look like after a proper cleaning?
  11. Would you like a free, no-obligation quote?
